html.p: An intuitive, full-featured JavaScript CRUD interface to the Splunk storage/passwords REST endpoint. Create, update, delete, and view credentials — plus manage permissions, sharing scope, and app context — all from a single dashboard.

To store passwords a user must have the admin_all_objects capability enabled within an assigned role. To read passwords a user must have the list_storage_passwords capability enabled within an assigned role. Grant list_storage_passwords carefully — users with this capability can view credentials across any app where they have read access.
The Credential Management dashboard provides a CRUD interface to create, update, and delete credentials.

Click + New Credential to reveal the creation form. Fill in a username, password, and optionally a realm (a descriptor such as prod or dev). The form pre-fills with secure defaults for owner, read users, write users, app scope, and sharing — update them before clicking Create.

Click any row in the table to expand it and reveal the inline update form. You can change the password, permissions, or app context. The realm cannot be changed after creation — this is a limitation of the storage/passwords REST endpoint, not the app.

Select one or more rows using the checkboxes in the left column, then click Delete. A confirmation dialog shows the credentials to be deleted before you confirm. You can also select all visible credentials using the checkbox in the table header.
Click the eye icon in the Password column to display the plain-text password in a modal dialog.
Type in the filter box at the top of the table to narrow results by username, realm, or app. The filter is case-insensitive and updates live as you type.
See the Splunk developer documentation for details on reading stored credentials from your app's Python or JavaScript code.
Please submit feature requests through GitHub using the enhancement label.
Please submit bugs through GitHub using the bug label.
